title: How The Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte (BMV:OMA B) Investment Story Is Being Rewritten
content:
Find your next quality investment with Simply Wall St's easy and powerful screener, trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte, de is in focus after analysts moved the fair value estimate from MX$259.46 to MX$259.89 and flagged a separate MX$35 price target cut. On one side, bullish voices link the smaller MX$8 uplift to refreshed assumptions that still leave room for upside. In contrast, more cautious analysts see the larger reduction as a signal that earlier expectations may have been too optimistic. As you read on, you will see how these mixed signals shape the evolving narrative and what to watch next in the updates.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte. de.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Barclays lifted its price target by MX$8 on 10 February 2026, which suggests the firm saw room to refine its assumptions and still support a slightly higher valuation for Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte, de. The MX$8 uplift indicates that, even after revisiting its model, Barclays viewed the shares as having potential relative to its prior stance. Some investors may read this as a sign of confidence in execution and cash generation potential.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

On 24 March 2026, Barclays cut its price target by MX$35, a far larger absolute move than the earlier uplift. This points to a more cautious update on the risk or return profile the firm now uses in its valuation work. The combination of a modest MX$8 increase followed by a MX$35 reduction highlights how sensitive the investment case can be to changes in Barclays assumptions on growth, capital needs or profitability. It also signals that forecasts may be under active review.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!BMV:OMA B 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 2 risks for Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte. de. See which could impact your investment.

What's in the News

Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte, de reported February 2026 operating results with total passengers of 2,016,352 compared with 1,913,133 a year earlier, and year-to-date passengers of 4,326,136 compared with 4,091,640 a year earlier. The company announced January 2026 operating results, recording total passengers of 2,309,784 compared with 2,178,507 in the same month a year earlier. Passenger traffic for the fourth quarter of 2025 was reported at 7.5 million, with the company also releasing full-year 2025 passenger traffic of 28.8 million with reference to 2024 levels.

Story Continues

How This Changes the Fair Value For Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte. de

Fair value moved from MX$259.46 to MX$259.89 in the updated estimate. Revenue growth assumption shifted from 11.39% to 12.55% for MX$ revenue. Net profit margin assumption moved from 38.91% to 36.41%. Future P/E multiple changed from 17.39x to 18.07x. Discount rate moved from 14.24% to 14.30% in the cash flow model.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives link a company's story to a financial forecast and fair value, so you can see how business trends, risks and expectations fit together. They refresh as new data, estimates and company updates come through.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte. de to stay up to date on:

How expansion and upgrades at Monterrey Airport and other key assets are expected to support aeronautical and commercial revenue over time. Why rising passenger volumes and higher commercial revenue per passenger, from areas like retail, food and beverage, VIP lounges and parking, are central to the earnings story. What concentrated exposure to Monterrey Airport, reliance on VivaAerobus and Volaris, upcoming tariff negotiations and leadership changes could mean for future revenue and margin stability.

title: Want to Play the Reshoring Theme? You Might Want to Buy These Stocks South of the Border.
content:
Reshoring is the macroeconomic theme of the United States, bringing industrial capacity from Asia (specifically China) back home to North and South America. Some manufacturing will be done in the United States (e.g., semiconductors), while some will flow to other nations.

No country is better positioned to benefit from reshoring than Mexico. But how does one invest to take advantage of the potential economic boom south of the border? The answer may lie in the aviation sector and the publicly traded airport stocks in Mexico.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »Image source: Getty Images.

Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico: Betting on Mexican tourism

Unlike in the United States, the operators of Mexican airports are publicly traded companies, of which there are three. They don't own the airport but have long-term (generally 50-year) contracts that allow them to manage these assets for the government while splitting the profits.

Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico(NYSE: PAC) is one of Mexico's airport operators, focused on the Pacific region, with its largest airports being in Guadalajara, Los Cabos, Tijuana, and Puerto Vallarta. These cities have a mix of international tourism and industrial capacity, with Tijuana benefiting from a land bridge connecting to San Diego that allows residents to easily get on cheaper flights when visiting Mexico.

The stock is trading down 15% from its highs due to fears over recent cartel violence in Mexico, which seems to have subsided for the time being, as well as global concerns about rising oil prices and their potential impact on air travel demand.

Airport operators such as Grupo Pacifico make money as more passengers flow through their airports, along with government-allowed price increases. Over the long term, air traffic to Mexico has exploded because of international tourism, general economic growth in Mexico, and growing industrial capacity in places such as Guadalajara, which drives business travel.

Right now, the stock trades at a dividend yield of 3.5% and at 13 times its trailing EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization). This is for a business that has grown its revenue by 286% over the past 10 years in U.S. dollar terms, despite a global pandemic in between. Unless you think Mexican tourism is going to fall apart overnight, now could be a great time to buy Grupo Pacifico stock.

Story Continues

Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte: The reshoring focus

The airport operator most focused on the reshoring theme is Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte(NASDAQ: OMAB), which operates the Monterrey airport and a number of smaller regional airports across Mexico's North and Western regions.

Monterrey is the wealthiest large city in Mexico and is closest to the United States. It has become wealthier than the average Mexican metro area because of its industrial might, with many companies around the world setting up shop there to ship goods to the United States. It even offers direct flights to Tokyo and Seoul to serve electronic manufacturing companies in the area.

It lacks the tourist charm, but Monterrey will benefit if the reshoring trend continues through the rest of this decade. Total passenger traffic grew 8.5% year over year in 2025, with Monterrey growing 15% and making up around half of passenger volume. Right now, the stock trades at a dividend yield of 4.2% and 11.5 times EBITDA, making it even cheaper than Grupo Pacifico.Data by YCharts.

Should you buy Mexican airport stocks?

If you have only invested in your home market, it might feel scary to buy a stock from a country like Mexico, where you have less understanding of the local culture and economy. This fear may be warranted for consumer goods, but airports are very similar, no matter which country you go to.

Currency risks are another factor that pops up, but they can be mitigated by increasing per-passenger fees and by international traffic and commercial revenue from tourism shops. As a monopoly, the Mexican government regulates airports and determines the prices inbound flights can charge, with Grupo Norte granted the right to increase prices by 38% over the next five years. That sounds like a good business to me.

Mexican airport stocks are well-positioned to deliver strong returns for shareholders over the next decade.

Should you buy stock in Grupo Aeroportuario Del Centro NorteB. De C.v. right now?

Before you buy stock in Grupo Aeroportuario Del Centro NorteB. De C.v., consider this:

The Motley Fool Stock Advisor analyst team just identified what they believe are the 10 best stocks for investors to buy now… and Grupo Aeroportuario Del Centro NorteB. De C.v. wasn’t one of them. The 10 stocks that made the cut could produce monster returns in the coming years.

Consider when Netflix made this list on December 17, 2004... if you invested $1,000 at the time of our recommendation, you’d have $555,526!* Or when Nvidia made this list on April 15, 2005... if you invested $1,000 at the time of our recommendation, you’d have $1,156,403!*

Now, it’s worth noting Stock Advisor’s total average return is 968% — a market-crushing outperformance compared to 191% for the S&P 500. Don't miss the latest top 10 list, available with Stock Advisor, and join an investing community built by individual investors for individual investors.

Brett Schafer has positions in Grupo Aeroportuario Del Centro NorteB. De C.v. The Motley Fool recommends Grupo Aeroportuario Del PacíficoB. De C.v. The Motley Fool has a disclosure policy.

title: Why The Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte (BMV:OMA B) Narrative Is Shifting On Valuation And Risk
content:
Make better investment decisions with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.

Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte has seen its assessed fair value move from MX$262.46 to MX$259.46, a small cut that still matters if you are tracking valuation closely. This shift comes as research houses pull in different directions, with some lifting price targets by MX$8 and others cutting by as much as MX$35. These differing moves reflect mixed views on execution risk and growth durability. In the sections that follow, you will see how these target moves fit together and what to watch as the story continues to evolve.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte. de.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Bradesco BBI upgraded Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte to Outperform from Neutral in early January, signaling greater confidence in how the company can execute on its current plan at the price level implied by its $120 target. The January upgrade suggests Bradesco BBI sees enough support for the long term growth story to justify a more constructive stance, even after accounting for operational and regulatory risks that are common in the sector.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Barclays has adjusted its view more cautiously, first lifting the OMA target by MX$8 in February, then cutting it by MX$35 in March. This highlights rising concern about execution risk and the strength of the growth runway at prior valuation levels. The recent Barclays reduction in the target price indicates a tighter margin for error, with less room for disappointments in traffic, capital spending or regulatory outcomes before the current valuation could come into question.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!BMV:OMA B 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 2 risks for Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte. de. See which could impact your investment.

What's in the News

Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte reported February 2026 operating results with 2,016,352 total passengers, compared with 1,913,133 a year earlier, and year-to-date passengers of 4,326,136 versus 4,091,640 a year earlier. For January 2026, the company reported 2,309,784 total passengers, compared with 2,178,507 in January 2025. Passenger traffic for 4Q25 was 6.0% higher than 4Q24 at 7.5 million passengers, and full-year 2025 passenger traffic was 8.5% higher at 28.8 million passengers. In December 2025, total passengers were 2,654,680 compared with 2,484,220 a year earlier, with year-to-date passengers of 28,750,694 versus 26,510,498 a year earlier.

Story Continues

How This Changes the Fair Value For Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte. de

Fair Value moved from MX$262.46 to MX$259.46, a reduction of about 1.1%. Revenue Growth moved from 11.01% to 11.39%. Net Profit Margin moved from 39.00% to 38.91%. Future P/E moved from 17.73x to 17.39x. Discount Rate moved from 14.23% to 14.24%.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives link a company’s business story to a financial forecast and a fair value view, so you can see how assumptions, risks and growth drivers fit together. They update over time as new data and research come through.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte. de to stay up to date on:

How investment in Monterrey Airport capacity and upgrades ties into long term expectations for passenger throughput and margins. The role of rising passenger volumes and higher commercial revenue per passenger in supporting earnings and cash flow assumptions. Key risks around heavy exposure to Monterrey, reliance on VivaAerobus and Volaris, regulatory tariff decisions and leadership changes that could affect execution.

title: Strength Seen in Universal Truckload (ULH): Can Its 12.3% Jump Turn into More Strength?
content:
Universal Logistics (ULH) shares ended the last trading session 12.3% higher at $19.84. The jump came on an impressive volume with a higher-than-average number of shares changing hands in the session. This compares to the stock's 5.5% gain over the past four weeks.

Shares of this transportation company have performed well so far this year, gaining 30%. Earlier this month, the company reported earnings of 14 cents per share, comparing favorably with the Zacks Consensus Estimate of a loss of 5 cents. Revenues of $385.4 million also surpassed the consensus mark. Management expects the strength and resilience of the company's business model to drive long-term success, despite the current challenging market environment..

This trucking and logistics company is expected to post quarterly earnings of $0.08 per share in its upcoming report, which represents a year-over-year change of -65.2%. Revenues are expected to be $382.3 million, unchanged compared to the year-ago quarter.

Earnings and revenue growth expectations certainly give a good sense of the potential strength in a stock, but empirical research shows that trends in earnings estimate revisions are strongly correlated with near-term stock price movements.

For Universal Truckload, the consensus EPS estimate for the quarter has been revised 14.3% higher over the last 30 days to the current level. And a positive trend in earnings estimate revision usually translates into price appreciation. So, make sure to keep an eye on ULH going forward to see if this recent jump can turn into more strength down the road.

The stock currently carries a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy). You can see the complete list of today's Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) stocks here >>>>

Universal Truckload is a member of the Zacks Transportation - Services industry. One other stock in the same industry, Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte (OMAB), finished the last trading session 0.1% higher at $108.82. OMAB has returned -19.5% over the past month.

For Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte, the consensus EPS estimate for the upcoming report has remained unchanged over the past month at $1.8. This represents a change of +38.5% from what the company reported a year ago. Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte currently has a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold).
